   265	
   266	/**
   267	 * Loads an ECDSA public key from a raw format for signature verification.
   268	 *
   269	 * @param {Uint8Array} publicKey - The raw P-256 public key bytes.
   270	 * @returns {ProjPointType<bigint>} - The parsed ECDSA public key.
   271	 * @throws {Error} - If the public key is invalid.
   272	 */
   273	const loadPublicKey = (publicKey: Uint8Array): ProjPointType<bigint> => {
   274	  return p256.ProjectivePoint.fromHex(uint8ArrayToHexString(publicKey));
   275	};
   276	
   277	/**
   278	 * Decodes a private key based on the specified format.
   279	 *
   280	 * @param {string} privateKey - The private key to decode.
   281	 * @param {string} keyFormat - The format of the private key (e.g., "SOLANA", "HEXADECIMAL").
   282	 * @returns {Uint8Array} - The decoded private key.
   283	 */
   284	const decodeKey = (privateKey: string, keyFormat: any): Uint8Array => {
   285	  switch (keyFormat) {
   286	    case "SOLANA":
   287	      const decodedKeyBytes = bs58.decode(privateKey);
   288	      if (decodedKeyBytes.length !== 64) {
   289	        throw new Error(
   290	          `invalid key length. Expected 64 bytes. Got ${decodedKeyBytes.length}.`,
   291	        );
   292	      }
   293	      return decodedKeyBytes.subarray(0, 32);
   294	    case "HEXADECIMAL":
   295	      if (privateKey.startsWith("0x")) {
   296	        return uint8ArrayFromHexString(privateKey.slice(2));
   297	      }
   298	      return uint8ArrayFromHexString(privateKey);
   299	    default:
   300	      console.warn(
   301	        `invalid key format: ${keyFormat}. Defaulting to HEXADECIMAL.`,
   302	      );
   303	      if (privateKey.startsWith("0x")) {
   304	        return uint8ArrayFromHexString(privateKey.slice(2));
   305	      }
   306	      return uint8ArrayFromHexString(privateKey);
   307	  }
   308	};
   309	
   310	/**
   311	 * Encrypts a private key bundle using HPKE and verifies the enclave signature.
   312	 *
   313	 * @param {EncryptPrivateKeyToBundleParams} params - An object containing the private key, key format, bundle, user, and organization details. Optionally, you can override the default signer key (for testing purposes)
   314	 * @returns {Promise<string>} - A promise that resolves to a JSON string representing the encrypted bundle.
   315	 * @throws {Error} - If enclave signature verification or any other validation fails.
   316	 */
   317	export const encryptPrivateKeyToBundle = async ({
   318	  privateKey,
   319	  keyFormat,
   320	  importBundle,
   321	  userId,
   322	  organizationId,
   323	  dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey,
   324	}: EncryptPrivateKeyToBundleParams): Promise<string> => {
   325	  const parsedImportBundle = JSON.parse(importBundle);
   326	  const plainTextBuf = decodeKey(privateKey, keyFormat);
   327	  const verified = await verifyEnclaveSignature(
   328	    parsedImportBundle.enclaveQuorumPublic,
   329	    parsedImportBundle.dataSignature,
   330	    parsedImportBundle.data,
   331	    dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey,
   332	  );
   333	  if (!verified) {
   334	    throw new Error(`failed to verify enclave signature: ${importBundle}`);
   335	  }
   336	
   337	  const signedData = JSON.parse(
   338	    new TextDecoder().decode(uint8ArrayFromHexString(parsedImportBundle.data)),
   339	  );
   340	
   341	  if (
   342	    !signedData.organizationId ||
   343	    signedData.organizationId !== organizationId
   344	  ) {
   345	    throw new Error(
   346	      `organization id does not match expected value. Expected: ${organizationId}. Found: ${signedData.organizationId}.`,
   347	    );
   348	  }
   349	  if (!signedData.userId || signedData.userId !== userId) {
   350	    throw new Error(
   351	      `user id does not match expected value. Expected: ${userId}. Found: ${signedData.userId}.`,
   352	    );
   353	  }
   354	
   355	  if (!signedData.targetPublic) {
   356	    throw new Error('missing "targetPublic" in bundle signed data');
   357	  }
   358	
   359	  // Load target public key generated from enclave
   360	  const targetKeyBuf = uint8ArrayFromHexString(signedData.targetPublic);
   361	  const privateKeyBundle = hpkeEncrypt({ plainTextBuf, targetKeyBuf });
   362	  return formatHpkeBuf(privateKeyBundle);
   363	};
   364	
   365	/**
   366	  /**
   367	   * Encrypts a mnemonic wallet bundle using HPKE and verifies the enclave signature.
   368	   *
   369	   * @param {EncryptWalletToBundleParams} params - An object containing the mnemonic, bundle, user, and organization details. Optionally, you can override the default signer key (for testing purposes).
   370	   * @returns {Promise<string>} - A promise that resolves to a JSON string representing the encrypted wallet bundle.
   371	   * @throws {Error} - If enclave signature verification or any other validation fails.
   372	   */
   373	export const encryptWalletToBundle = async ({
   374	  mnemonic,
   375	  importBundle,
   376	  userId,
   377	  organizationId,
   378	  dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey,
   379	}: EncryptWalletToBundleParams): Promise<string> => {
   380	  const parsedImportBundle = JSON.parse(importBundle);
   381	  const plainTextBuf = new TextEncoder().encode(mnemonic);
   382	  const verified = await verifyEnclaveSignature(
   383	    parsedImportBundle.enclaveQuorumPublic,
   384	    parsedImportBundle.dataSignature,
   385	    parsedImportBundle.data,
   386	    dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey,
   387	  );
   388	  if (!verified) {
   389	    throw new Error(`failed to verify enclave signature: ${importBundle}`);
   390	  }
   391	
   392	  const signedData = JSON.parse(
   393	    new TextDecoder().decode(uint8ArrayFromHexString(parsedImportBundle.data)),
   394	  );
   395	
   396	  if (
   397	    !signedData.organizationId ||
   398	    signedData.organizationId !== organizationId
   399	  ) {
   400	    throw new Error(
   401	      `organization id does not match expected value. Expected: ${organizationId}. Found: ${signedData.organizationId}.`,
   402	    );
   403	  }
   404	  if (!signedData.userId || signedData.userId !== userId) {
   405	    throw new Error(
   406	      `user id does not match expected value. Expected: ${userId}. Found: ${signedData.userId}.`,
   407	    );
   408	  }
   409	
   410	  if (!signedData.targetPublic) {
   411	    throw new Error('missing "targetPublic" in bundle signed data');
   412	  }
   413	
   414	  // Load target public key generated from enclave
   415	  const targetKeyBuf = uint8ArrayFromHexString(signedData.targetPublic);
   416	  const privateKeyBundle = hpkeEncrypt({ plainTextBuf, targetKeyBuf });
   417	  return formatHpkeBuf(privateKeyBundle);
   418	};
   419	
   420	/**
   421	 * Verifies that a **session JWT** was signed by Turnkey’s
   422	 * notarizer key (P-256 / ES256, compact 64-byte r‖s signature).
   423	 *
   424	 * How it works
   425	 * ------------
   426	 * 1.  Split the JWT into `header.payload.signature`.
   427	 * 2.  **Double-hash** the string `"header.payload"`:
   428	 *        `h1 = sha256(header.payload)`
   429	 *        `msg = sha256(h1)`
   430	 *     (The Rust signer feeds `h1` into `SigningKey::sign`, which hashes once
   431	 *     more internally, yielding `msg`.)
   432	 * 3.  Base64-URL-decode the signature (`r||s`, 64 bytes).
   433	 * 4.  Import the notarizer public key (hex `04‖X‖Y` → `Uint8Array`).
   434	 * 5.  Call `p256.verify(signature, msg, publicKey)`; noble treats the 32-byte
   435	 *     `msg` as a pre-hashed digest and performs ECDSA verification.
   436	 *
   437	 * @param jwt   The session JWT to validate.
   438	 * @param dangerouslyOverrideNotarizerPublicKey *(optional)* Hex-encoded
   439	 *              uncompressed P-256 public key to verify against (use only in
   440	 *              tests).  Defaults to the production notarizer key.
   441	 * @returns `true` if the signature is valid for the given key, else `false`.
   442	 * @throws  If the JWT is malformed.
   443	 */
   444	export const verifySessionJwtSignature = async (
   445	  jwt: string,
   446	  dangerouslyOverrideNotarizerPublicKey?: string,
   447	): Promise<boolean> => {
   448	  const notarizerKeyHex =
   449	    dangerouslyOverrideNotarizerPublicKey ??
   450	    PRODUCTION_NOTARIZER_SIGN_PUBLIC_KEY;
   451	
   452	  /* 1. split JWT -------------------------------------------------------- */
   453	  const [headerB64, payloadB64, signatureB64] = jwt.split(".");
   454	  if (!signatureB64) throw new Error("invalid JWT: need 3 parts");
   455	  const signingInput = `${headerB64}.${payloadB64}`;
   456	
   457	  /* 2. sha256(sha256(header.payload)) ----------------------------------- */
   458	  const h1 = sha256(new TextEncoder().encode(signingInput));
   459	  const msgDigest = sha256(h1); // 32-byte Uint8Array
   460	
   461	  /* 3. base64-url decode signature -------------------------------------- */
   462	  const toB64 = (u: string) =>
   463	    (u = u.replace(/-/g, "+").replace(/_/g, "/")).padEnd(
   464	      u.length + ((4 - (u.length % 4)) % 4),
   465	      "=",
   466	    );
   467	  const signature = Uint8Array.from(
   468	    atob(toB64(signatureB64))
   469	      .split("")
   470	      .map((c) => c.charCodeAt(0)),
   471	  ); // 64 bytes
   472	
   473	  /* 4. load public key -------------------------------------------------- */
   474	  const publicKey = uint8ArrayFromHexString(notarizerKeyHex);
   475	
   476	  /* 5. verify ----------------------------------------------------------- */
   477	  return p256.verify(signature, msgDigest, publicKey);
   478	};
   479	
   480	/**
   481	 * Encrypts a message to an uncompressed P256 public key
   482	 * The function takes in standard strings and converts them
   483	 * to Uint8Arrays to be used by the lower level quorumKeyEncrypt
   484	 * function. More details about how the encryption works is described
   485	 * in that function's documentation.
   486	 *
   487	 * @param targetPublicKeyUncompressed A hex string uncompressed public key to encrypt a message to
   488	 * @param message A standard string message to encrypt, does not have to be hex encoded
   489	 * @returns {Promise<Uint8Array>} A borsh serialized envelope with the encrypted message (more details found in quorumKeyEncrypt)
   490	 */
   491	export const encryptToEnclave = async (
   492	  targetPublicKeyUncompressed: string,
   493	  message: string,
   494	): Promise<Uint8Array> => {
   495	  return await quorumKeyEncrypt(
   496	    uint8ArrayFromHexString(targetPublicKeyUncompressed),
   497	    new TextEncoder().encode(message),
   498	  );
   499	};
   500	
   501	/**
   502	 * Helper function used specifically to encrypt a client secret to
   503	 * TLS Fetchers quorum key. This is used for client_secret upload
   504	 * when enabling authentication with an OAuth 2.0 provider
   505	 *
   506	 * @param client_secret The client secret issued by the OAuth 2.0 provider
   507	 * @param dangerouslyOverrideTlsFetcherPublicKey *(optional)* Hex-encoded
   508	 *              uncompressed P-256 public key to encrypt to (use only in
   509	 *              tests/dev environment).  Defaults to the production TLS Fetcher key.
   510	 * @returns {Promise<string>} A hex encoded borsh serialized envelope with the encrypted client
   511	 *                            secret meant to be passed to the CreateOauth2Credential Activity
   512	 */
   513	export const encryptOauth2ClientSecret = async (
   514	  client_secret: string,
   515	  dangerouslyOverrideTlsFetcherPublicKey?: string,
   516	): Promise<string> => {
   517	  return uint8ArrayToHexString(
   518	    await encryptToEnclave(
   519	      dangerouslyOverrideTlsFetcherPublicKey ??
   520	        PRODUCTION_TLS_FETCHER_ENCRYPT_PUBLIC_KEY,
   521	      client_secret,
   522	    ),
   523	  );
   524	};
   525	
   526	/**
   527	 * Helper function used specifically to encrypt your on ramp private/secret api keys
   528	 * to the on ramp encryption public key. This is used before uploading your on ramp
   529	 * credentials to Turnkey via the CreateFiatOnRampCredential activity
   530	 *
   531	 * @param secret The private/secret api key issued by the on ramp provider
   532	 * @param dangerouslyOverrideOnRampEncryptionPublicKey *(optional)* Hex-encoded
   533	 *              uncompressed P-256 public key to encrypt to (use only in
   534	 *              tests/dev environment).  Defaults to the production on ramp encryption public key.
   535	 * @returns {Promise<string>} A base58check encoded borsh serialized envelope with the encrypted secret
   536	 *                            meant to be passed to the CreateFiatOnRampCredential activity
   537	 */
   538	export const encryptOnRampSecret = (
   539	  secret: string,
   540	  dangerouslyOverrideOnRampEncryptionPublicKey?: string,
   541	): string => {
   542	  return bs58check.encode(
   543	    hpkeEncrypt({
   544	      plainTextBuf: new TextEncoder().encode(secret),
   545	      targetKeyBuf: uncompressRawPublicKey(
   546	        uint8ArrayFromHexString(
   547	          dangerouslyOverrideOnRampEncryptionPublicKey ??
   548	            PRODUCTION_ON_RAMP_CREDENTIALS_ENCRYPTION_PUBLIC_KEY,
   549	        ),
   550	      ),
   551	    }),
   552	  );
   553	};

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Compute the modular square root using the Tonelli-Shanks algorithm.
     3	 */
     4	export const modSqrt = (x: bigint, p: bigint): bigint => {
     5	  if (p <= BigInt(0)) {
     6	    throw new Error("p must be positive");
     7	  }
     8	  const base = x % p;
     9	
    10	  // Check if p % 4 == 3 (applies to NIST curves P-256, P-384, and P-521)
    11	  if (testBit(p, 0) && testBit(p, 1)) {
    12	    const q = (p + BigInt(1)) >> BigInt(2);
    13	    const squareRoot = modPow(base, q, p);
    14	    if ((squareRoot * squareRoot) % p !== base) {
    15	      throw new Error("could not find a modular square root");
    16	    }
    17	    return squareRoot;
    18	  }
    19	
    20	  // Other elliptic curve types not supported
    21	  throw new Error("unsupported modulus value");
    22	};
    23	
    24	/**
    25	 * Test if a specific bit is set.
    26	 */
    27	export const testBit = (n: bigint, i: number): boolean => {
    28	  const m = BigInt(1) << BigInt(i);
    29	  return (n & m) !== BigInt(0);
    30	};
    31	
    32	/**
    33	 * Compute the modular exponentiation.
    34	 */
    35	const modPow = (b: bigint, exp: bigint, p: bigint): bigint => {
    36	  if (exp === BigInt(0)) {
    37	    return BigInt(1);
    38	  }
    39	  let result = b % p;
    40	  const exponentBitString = exp.toString(2);
    41	  for (let i = 1; i < exponentBitString.length; ++i) {
    42	    result = (result * result) % p;
    43	    if (exponentBitString[i] === "1") {
    44	      result = (result * b) % p;
    45	    }
    46	  }
    47	  return result;
    48	};

   648	        /// <summary>
   649	        /// Decrypt a Turnkey credential bundle.
   650	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>turnkey.ts decryptCredentialBundle</c>.
   651	        /// </summary>
   652	        /// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">
   653	        /// Wraps any decryption / parse failure with the upstream message
   654	        /// <c>"Error decrypting bundle: ..."</c>.
   655	        /// </exception>
   656	        public static string DecryptCredentialBundle(string encryptedCredentialBundle, string embeddedKey)
   657	        {
   658	            if (encryptedCredentialBundle == null)
   659	                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(encryptedCredentialBundle));
   660	            if (embeddedKey == null)
   661	                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(embeddedKey));
   662	            try
   663	            {
   664	                // Upstream uses bs58check.decode exclusively (NO raw bs58 fallback).
   665	                var bundleBytes = Encoding.Base58CheckDecode(encryptedCredentialBundle);
   666	                if (bundleBytes.Length <= 33)
   667	                {
   668	                    throw new InvalidOperationException(
   669	                        "Bundle size " + bundleBytes.Length
   670	                        + " is too low. Expecting a compressed public key (33 bytes) and an encrypted credential.");
   671	                }
   672	
   673	                var compressedEncappedKeyBuf = new byte[33];
   674	                Array.Copy(bundleBytes, 0, compressedEncappedKeyBuf, 0, 33);
   675	                var ciphertextBuf = new byte[bundleBytes.Length - 33];
   676	                Array.Copy(bundleBytes, 33, ciphertextBuf, 0, ciphertextBuf.Length);
   677	
   678	                var encappedKeyBuf = UncompressRawPublicKey(compressedEncappedKeyBuf);
   679	                var decryptedData = HpkeDecrypt(new HpkeDecryptParams
   680	                {
   681	                    CiphertextBuf = ciphertextBuf,
   682	                    EncappedKeyBuf = encappedKeyBuf,
   683	                    ReceiverPriv = embeddedKey,
   684	                });
   685	                return Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(decryptedData);
   686	            }
   687	            catch (Exception error)
   688	            {
   689	                throw new InvalidOperationException(
   690	                    "\"Error decrypting bundle:\", " + error.Message, error);
   691	            }
   692	        }
   693	
   694	        /// <summary>
   695	        /// Encrypt a private key into a Turnkey import bundle.
   696	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>turnkey.ts encryptPrivateKeyToBundle</c>.
   697	        /// </summary>
   698	        public static string EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle(EncryptPrivateKeyToBundleParams parameters)
   699	        {
   700	            if (parameters == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters));
   701	
   702	            // Upstream order, exactly:
   703	            //   1. JSON.parse(importBundle)
   704	            //   2. plainTextBuf = decodeKey(privateKey, keyFormat)
   705	            //   3. verifyEnclaveSignature(...)
   706	            //   4. parse signed data, check organizationId / userId / targetPublic
   707	            //   5. hpkeEncrypt + formatHpkeBuf
   708	            // We delegate "required string" failures to the upstream operations
   709	            // (JsonDocument.Parse, DecodeKey hex parse, etc.) instead of
   710	            // pre-validating, so the error precedence matches upstream.
   711	            if (parameters.ImportBundle == null)
   712	            {
   713	                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters.ImportBundle));
   714	            }
   715	            if (parameters.PrivateKey == null)
   716	            {
   717	                throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(parameters.PrivateKey));
   718	            }

   720	            var parsedImportBundle = bundleDoc.RootElement;

   723

   725	            string? dataSignature = GetStringOrNull(parsedImportBundle, "dataSignature");
   726	            string? signedDataHex = GetStringOrNull(parsedImportBundle, "data");

   729	                enclaveQuorumPublic,

   745	            {
   746	                throw new InvalidOperationException(

   753	            {
   754	                throw new InvalidOperationException(
   755	                    "user id does not match expected value. Expected: " + parameters.UserId
   756	                    + ". Found: " + userId + ".");
   757	            }

   759	            string? targetPublic = GetStringOrNull(signedData, "targetPublic");
   760	            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(targetPublic))
   761	            {
   762	                throw new InvalidOperationException("missing \"targetPublic\" in bundle signed data");
   763	            }

   767	            {
   768	                PlainTextBuf = plainTextBuf,
   769	                TargetKeyBuf = targetKeyBuf,
   770	            });

   798	                string? dataSignature = GetStringOrNull(parsedExportBundle, "dataSignature");
   799	                string? dataHex = GetStringOrNull(parsedExportBundle, "data");

   810	                }
   811	
   812	                var signedDataBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(dataHex!);
   813	                using var signedDoc = JsonDocument.Parse(System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetString(signedDataBytes));
   814	                var signedData = signedDoc.RootElement;
   815	
   816	                string? bundleOrgId = GetStringOrNull(signedData, "organizationId");
   817	                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(bundleOrgId)
   818	                    || !string.Equals(bundleOrgId, parameters.OrganizationId, StringComparison.Ordinal))
   819	                {
   820	                    throw new InvalidOperationException(
   821	                        "organization id does not match expected value. Expected: " + parameters.OrganizationId
   822	                        + ". Found: " + bundleOrgId + ".");
   823	                }
   824	
   825	                string? encappedPublic = GetStringOrNull(signedData, "encappedPublic");
   826	                if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(encappedPublic))
   827	                {
   828	                    throw new InvalidOperationException("missing \"encappedPublic\" in bundle signed data");
   829	                }
   830	                string? ciphertextStr = GetStringOrNull(signedData, "ciphertext");
   831	
   832	                var encappedKeyBuf = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(encappedPublic!);
   833	                var ciphertextBuf = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(ciphertextStr!);

   835	                var decryptedData = HpkeDecrypt(new HpkeDecryptParams
   836	                {
   837	                    CiphertextBuf = ciphertextBuf,
   838	                    EncappedKeyBuf = encappedKeyBuf,
   839	                    ReceiverPriv = parameters.EmbeddedKey,
   840	                });
   841

   843	                    && !parameters.ReturnMnemonic)

   851	                    var ed25519PrivKey = new Ed25519PrivateKeyParameters(decryptedData, 0);

   871	            }
   872	        }
   873	
   874	        /// <summary>
   875	        /// Verify the ECDSA signature on a Turnkey session JWT.
   876	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>turnkey.ts verifySessionJwtSignature</c>.
   877	        /// </summary>
   878	        /// <param name="jwt">The JWT to verify.</param>
   879	        /// <param name="dangerouslyOverrideNotarizerPublicKey">
   880	        /// Optional hex-encoded uncompressed P-256 public key to verify
   881	        /// against. Use for tests only; defaults to the production
   882	        /// notarizer key.
   883	        /// </param>
   884	        /// <returns><c>true</c> if the signature is valid for the given key.</returns>
   885	        /// <exception cref="InvalidOperationException">Thrown when the JWT is malformed (missing parts).</exception>
   886	        public static bool VerifySessionJwtSignature(
   887	            string jwt,
   888	            string? dangerouslyOverrideNotarizerPublicKey = null)
   889	        {
   890	            if (jwt == null) throw new ArgumentNullException(nameof(jwt));
   891	
   892	            string notarizerKeyHex =
   893	                dangerouslyOverrideNotarizerPublicKey ?? Constants.PRODUCTION_NOTARIZER_SIGN_PUBLIC_KEY;
   894	
   895	            var parts = jwt.Split('.');
   896	            // Upstream: const [a, b, sig] = jwt.split("."); if (!sig) throw new Error(...)
   897	            if (parts.Length < 3 || string.IsNullOrEmpty(parts[2]))
   898	            {
   899	                throw new InvalidOperationException("invalid JWT: need 3 parts");
   900	            }
   901	
   902	            string headerB64 = parts[0];
   903	            string payloadB64 = parts[1];
   904	            string signatureB64 = parts[2];
   905	            string signingInput = headerB64 + "." + payloadB64;
   906	
   907	            byte[] msgDigest;
   908	            using (var sha256 = SHA256.Create())
   909	            {
   910	                var h1 = sha256.ComputeHash(System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(signingInput));
   911	                msgDigest = sha256.ComputeHash(h1);
   912	            }
   913	
   914	            byte[] signature = Base64UrlDecode(signatureB64);
   915	            var publicKey = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(notarizerKeyHex);
   916	            return VerifyP256RawSignature(publicKey, signature, msgDigest);
   917	        }
   918	
   919	        #endregion
   920	
   921	        #region Private helpers
   922	
   923	        private static byte[] DeriveSS(byte[] encappedKeyBuf, string privHex)
   924	        {
   925	            var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
   926	            var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
   927	
   928	            var privBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(privHex);
   929	            var d = new BigInteger(1, privBytes);
   930	            var privateKeyParams = new ECPrivateKeyParameters(d, domainParams);
   931	
   932	            var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(encappedKeyBuf);
   933	            var publicKeyParams = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);
   934	
   935	            var agreement = new ECDHBasicAgreement();
   936	            agreement.Init(privateKeyParams);
   937	            var sharedSecretBig = agreement.CalculateAgreement(publicKeyParams);
   938	            var ss = sharedSecretBig.ToByteArrayUnsigned();
   939	
   940	            if (ss.Length < 32)
   941	            {
   942	                var padded = new byte[32];
   943	                Array.Copy(ss, 0, padded, 32 - ss.Length, ss.Length);
   944	                ss = padded;
   945	            }
   946	            return ss;
   947	        }
   948	
   949	        private static byte[] GetKemContext(byte[] encappedKeyBuf, string publicKey)
   950	        {
   951	            return Encoding.ConcatUint8Arrays(
   952	                encappedKeyBuf,
   953	                Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(publicKey));
   954	        }
   955	
   956	        private static byte[] BuildLabeledIkm(byte[] label, byte[] ikm, byte[] suiteId)
   957	        {
   958	            return Encoding.ConcatUint8Arrays(Constants.HPKE_VERSION, suiteId, label, ikm);
   959	        }
   960	
   961	        private static byte[] BuildLabeledInfo(byte[] label, byte[] info, byte[] suiteId, int len)
   962	        {
   963	            const int suiteIdStartIndex = 9;
   964	            var ret = new byte[suiteIdStartIndex + suiteId.Length + label.Length + info.Length];
   965	
   966	            ret[0] = 0;
   967	            ret[1] = (byte)len;
   968	
   969	            Array.Copy(Constants.HPKE_VERSION, 0, ret, 2, Constants.HPKE_VERSION.Length);
   970	            Array.Copy(suiteId, 0, ret, suiteIdStartIndex, suiteId.Length);
   971	            Array.Copy(label, 0, ret, suiteIdStartIndex + suiteId.Length, label.Length);
   972	            Array.Copy(info, 0, ret, suiteIdStartIndex + suiteId.Length + label.Length, info.Length);
   973	
   974	            return ret;
   975	        }
   976	
   977	        private static byte[] ExtractAndExpand(byte[] sharedSecret, byte[] ikm, byte[] info, int len)
   978	        {
   979	            var prk = Hkdf.Extract(sharedSecret, ikm);
   980	            return Hkdf.Expand(prk, info, len);
   981	        }
   982	
   983	        private static byte[] AesGcmDecrypt(byte[] encryptedData, byte[] key, byte[] iv, byte[] aad)
   984	        {
   985	            var cipher = new GcmBlockCipher(new AesEngine());
   986	            cipher.Init(false, new AeadParameters(new KeyParameter(key), 128, iv, aad));
   987	
   988	            var output = new byte[cipher.GetOutputSize(encryptedData.Length)];
   989	            int len = cipher.ProcessBytes(encryptedData, 0, encryptedData.Length, output, 0);
   990	            cipher.DoFinal(output, len);
   991	            return output;
   992	        }
   993	
   994	        private static byte[] AesGcmEncrypt(byte[] plainData, byte[] key, byte[] iv, byte[] aad)
   995	        {
   996	            var cipher = new GcmBlockCipher(new AesEngine());
   997	            cipher.Init(true, new AeadParameters(new KeyParameter(key), 128, iv, aad));
   998	
   999	            var output = new byte[cipher.GetOutputSize(plainData.Length)];
  1000	            int len = cipher.ProcessBytes(plainData, 0, plainData.Length, output, 0);
  1001	            cipher.DoFinal(output, len);
  1002	            return output;
  1003	        }
  1004	
  1005	        private static byte[] Base64UrlDecode(string input)
  1006	        {
  1007	            var output = input.Replace('-', '+').Replace('_', '/');
  1008	            switch (output.Length % 4)
  1009	            {
  1010	                case 2: output += "=="; break;
  1011	                case 3: output += "="; break;
  1012	            }
  1013	            return Convert.FromBase64String(output);
  1014	        }
  1015	
  1016	        private static bool VerifyP256RawSignature(byte[] publicKeyBytes, byte[] signatureRaw, byte[] messageDigest)
  1017	        {
  1018	            // Upstream noble p256.verify accepts r||s of length 64. Anything
  1019	            // else is an invalid signature; return false.
  1020	            if (signatureRaw.Length != 64)
  1021	            {
  1022	                return false;
  1023	            }
  1024	            try
  1025	            {
  1026	                var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
  1027	                var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
  1028	
  1029	                var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(publicKeyBytes);
  1030	                var publicKeyParams = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);
  1031	
  1032	                var r = new BigInteger(1, signatureRaw, 0, 32);
  1033	                var s = new BigInteger(1, signatureRaw, 32, 32);
  1034	                byte[] derSignature = new DerSequence(new DerInteger(r), new DerInteger(s)).GetDerEncoded();
  1035	
  1036	                var signer = SignerUtilities.GetSigner("NONEwithECDSA");
  1037	                signer.Init(false, publicKeyParams);
  1038	                signer.BlockUpdate(messageDigest, 0, messageDigest.Length);
  1039	                return signer.VerifySignature(derSignature);
  1040	            }
  1041	            catch
  1042	            {
  1043	                return false;
  1044	            }
  1045	        }
  1046	
  1047	        /// <summary>
  1048	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>turnkey.ts verifyEnclaveSignature</c>.
  1049	        /// Returns <c>true</c> when the signature verifies, <c>false</c>
  1050	        /// when the underlying ECDSA verify fails. Throws on mismatched
  1051	        /// signer key (matches upstream "expected signer key ... does not
  1052	        /// match signer key from bundle").
  1053	        /// </summary>
  1054	        private static bool VerifyEnclaveSignature(
  1055	            string? enclaveQuorumPublic,
  1056	            string? signatureHex,
  1057	            string? signedDataHex,
  1058	            string? dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey)
  1059	        {
  1060	            string expectedSignerPublicKey =
  1061	                dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey ?? Constants.PRODUCTION_SIGNER_SIGN_PUBLIC_KEY;
  1062	
  1063	            if (!string.Equals(enclaveQuorumPublic, expectedSignerPublicKey, StringComparison.Ordinal))
  1064	            {
  1065	                throw new InvalidOperationException(
  1066	                    "expected signer key " + expectedSignerPublicKey
  1067	                    + " does not match signer key from bundle: " + enclaveQuorumPublic);
  1068	            }
  1069	
  1070	            // Upstream returns false when the verify call fails; the caller
  1071	            // composes the contextual error message.
  1072	            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(signatureHex) || string.IsNullOrEmpty(signedDataHex))
  1073	            {
  1074	                return false;
  1075	            }
  1076	
  1077	            try
  1078	            {
  1079	                var publicKeyBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(expectedSignerPublicKey);
  1080	                var signatureBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(signatureHex!);
  1081	                var messageBytes = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(signedDataHex!);
  1082	
  1083	                var curve = ECNamedCurveTable.GetByName(CryptoConstants.CURVE_NAME);
  1084	                var domainParams = new ECDomainParameters(curve.Curve, curve.G, curve.N, curve.H, curve.GetSeed());
  1085	                var point = curve.Curve.DecodePoint(publicKeyBytes);
  1086	                var publicKeyParams = new ECPublicKeyParameters(point, domainParams);
  1087	
  1088	                var signer = SignerUtilities.GetSigner("SHA-256withECDSA");
  1089	                signer.Init(false, publicKeyParams);
  1090	                signer.BlockUpdate(messageBytes, 0, messageBytes.Length);
  1091	
  1092	                return signer.VerifySignature(signatureBytes);
  1093	            }
  1094	            catch
  1095	            {
  1096	                return false;
  1097	            }
  1098	        }
  1099	
  1100	        /// <summary>
  1101	        /// Equivalent to upstream <c>turnkey.ts decodeKey</c>. Default and
  1102	        /// unknown <paramref name="keyFormat"/> values fall back to hex
  1103	        /// parsing exactly like upstream <c>default:</c> branch.
  1104	        /// </summary>
  1105	        private static byte[] DecodeKey(string privateKey, string? keyFormat)
  1106	        {
  1107	            if (string.Equals(keyFormat, "SOLANA", StringComparison.Ordinal))
  1108	            {
  1109	                var decoded = Encoding.Base58Decode(privateKey);
  1110	                if (decoded.Length != 64)
  1111	                {
  1112	                    throw new InvalidOperationException(
  1113	                        "invalid key length. Expected 64 bytes. Got " + decoded.Length + ".");
  1114	                }
  1115	                var first32 = new byte[32];
  1116	                Array.Copy(decoded, 0, first32, 0, 32);
  1117	                return first32;
  1118	            }
  1119	            // HEXADECIMAL (and unknown fallback). Upstream "default:" branch
  1120	            // also accepts the unknown case by falling through to hex parsing
  1121	            // (it logs a warn; this port silently matches the behavior).
  1122	            string normalized = privateKey.StartsWith("0x", StringComparison.Ordinal)
  1123	                ? privateKey.Substring(2)
  1124	                : privateKey;
  1125	            return Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(normalized);
  1126	        }
  1127	
  1128	        private static string? GetStringOrNull(JsonElement element, string propertyName)
  1129	        {
  1130	            if (element.ValueKind != JsonValueKind.Object)
  1131	            {
  1132	                return null;
  1133	            }
  1134	            if (!element.TryGetProperty(propertyName, out var value))
  1135	            {
  1136	                return null;
  1137	            }
  1138	            if (value.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Null || value.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.Undefined)
  1139	            {
  1140	                return null;
  1141	            }
  1142	            return value.ValueKind == JsonValueKind.String ? value.GetString() : null;
  1143	        }
  1144	
  1145	        #endregion
  1146	    }
  1147	}

     1	// CryptoTests.cs — xunit tests for src/Crypto.cs.
     2	//
     3	// Vector sources:
     4	//   - HKDF RFC 5869 test cases A.1..A.3 (the SHA-256 cases)
     5	//   - NIST P-256 known points (G and 2G) for GetPublicKey

     7	//   - HpkeEncrypt/HpkeDecrypt roundtrip (deterministic round-trip)

    12	// are not unit-testable without a real Turnkey signing key. They are

    16	using System;
    17	using System.Text;
    18	using FluentAssertions;

    22	namespace Turnkey.Tests

    24	    public class CryptoTests

    26	        // ============================================================
    27	        // Math.ModSqrt — Tonelli-Shanks
    28	        // ============================================================
    29	
    30	        [Fact]
    31	        public void ModSqrt_SmallPrime_HappyPath()
    32	        {
    33	            // 4 = 2^2 mod 7, ModSqrt(4, 7) should be 2 or 5 (-2 mod 7).
    34	            var p = new BigInteger("7");
    35	            var x = new BigInteger("4");
    36	            var root = Crypto.Math.ModSqrt(x, p);
    37	            (root.Equals(new BigInteger("2")) || root.Equals(new BigInteger("5")))
    38	                .Should().BeTrue($"got {root}");
    39	        }
    40	
    41	        [Fact]
    42	        public void ModSqrt_NonResidue_Throws()
    43	        {
    44	            // 3 is a non-residue mod 7.
    45	            var p = new BigInteger("7");
    46	            var x = new BigInteger("3");
    47	            Action act = () => Crypto.Math.ModSqrt(x, p);
    48	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
    49	               .WithMessage("could not find a modular square root");
    50	        }
    51	
    52	        [Fact]
    53	        public void ModSqrt_NonPositiveP_Throws()
    54	        {
    55	            var p = new BigInteger("0");
    56	            var x = new BigInteger("1");
    57	            Action act = () => Crypto.Math.ModSqrt(x, p);
    58	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
    59	               .WithMessage("p must be positive");
    60	        }
    61	
    62	        [Fact]
    63	        public void ModSqrt_P256Prime_Works()
    64	        {
    65	            // P-256 prime. Pick any quadratic residue: 4 = 2^2.
    66	            var p = new BigInteger(CryptoConstants.P256_P);
    67	            var x = new BigInteger("4");
    68	            var root = Crypto.Math.ModSqrt(x, p);
    69	            root.Multiply(root).Mod(p).Equals(x).Should().BeTrue();
    70	        }

   180	        // ============================================================

   183	
   184	        [Fact]
   185	        public void CompressUncompress_Roundtrip_RandomKeys()
   186	        {
   187	            for (int i = 0; i < 8; i++)
   188	            {
   189	                var kp = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   190	                byte[] uncompressed = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(kp.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   191	                byte[] compressed = Crypto.CompressRawPublicKey(uncompressed);
   192	                compressed.Should().HaveCount(33);
   193	                compressed[0].Should().BeOneOf((byte)0x02, (byte)0x03);
   194

   196	                recovered.Should().Equal(uncompressed);

   200	        [Fact]
   201	        public void CompressRawPublicKey_Permissive_OnAnyLength()
   202	        {
   203	            // Upstream behavior: slice + LSB-flip regardless of input shape.
   204	            // For a 65-byte uncompressed key we produce a 33-byte compressed key.
   205	            // For other lengths we produce a different-length output that
   206	            // upstream would also produce. Confirm we no longer throw on
   207	            // malformed input.
   208	            var raw = new byte[64];
   209	            raw[0] = 0x04;
   210	            raw[63] = 0x01;
   211	            Action act = () => Crypto.CompressRawPublicKey(raw);
   212	            act.Should().NotThrow();

   235	        // ============================================================

   237	        // ============================================================
   238	
   239	        [Fact]
   240	        public void GenerateP256KeyPair_LengthsAreCorrect()
   241	        {
   242	            var kp = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   243	            kp.PrivateKey.Should().HaveLength(64);          // 32 bytes hex
   244	            kp.PublicKey.Should().HaveLength(66);            // 33 bytes hex
   245	            kp.PublicKeyUncompressed.Should().HaveLength(130); // 65 bytes hex
   246	            kp.PublicKey.Should().MatchRegex("^0[23][0-9a-f]{64}$");
   247	            kp.PublicKeyUncompressed.Should().StartWith("04");

   249	
   250	        [Fact]
   251	        public void GetPublicKey_FromPrivateKey_ProducesCompressedByDefault()
   252	        {
   253	            var kp = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   254	            byte[] priv = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(kp.PrivateKey);
   255	            byte[] pub = Crypto.GetPublicKey(priv); // isCompressed default true
   256	            pub.Should().HaveCount(33);
   257	            Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(pub).Should().Be(kp.PublicKey);
   258	        }
   259	
   260	        [Fact]
   261	        public void GetPublicKey_Uncompressed_MatchesGenerator()
   262	        {
   263	            var kp = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   264	            byte[] priv = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(kp.PrivateKey);
   265	            byte[] uncompressed = Crypto.GetPublicKey(priv, isCompressed: false);
   266	            Encoding.Uint8ArrayToHexString(uncompressed).Should().Be(kp.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   267	        }
   268	
   269	        [Fact]
   270	        public void GetPublicKey_HexStringOverload_MatchesByteOverload()
   271	        {
   272	            var kp = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   273	            byte[] viaBytes = Crypto.GetPublicKey(Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(kp.PrivateKey));
   274	            byte[] viaHex = Crypto.GetPublicKey(kp.PrivateKey);
   275	            viaBytes.Should().Equal(viaHex);
   276	        }
   277	
   278	        // ============================================================
   279	        // HPKE roundtrip
   280	        // ============================================================
   281	
   282	        [Fact]
   283	        public void Hpke_EncryptThenDecrypt_RoundTripsArbitraryPayload()
   284	        {
   285	            // Receiver key pair.
   286	            var recv = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   287	            byte[] recvPubUncompressed = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(recv.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   288	
   289	            byte[] payload = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(
   290	                "deadbeefcafebabe1234567890abcdef00112233445566778899aabbccddeeff");
   291	
   292	            byte[] encrypted = Crypto.HpkeEncrypt(new Crypto.HpkeEncryptParams
   293	            {
   294	                PlainTextBuf = payload,
   295	                TargetKeyBuf = recvPubUncompressed,
   296	            });
   297	
   298	            // encrypted = compressed(senderPub) || ciphertext (33 + N bytes)
   299	            encrypted.Length.Should().BeGreaterThan(33);
   300	
   301	            // Split, uncompress the sender's compressed point, then decrypt.
   302	            var compressedSender = new byte[33];
   303	            Array.Copy(encrypted, 0, compressedSender, 0, 33);
   304	            var ciphertext = new byte[encrypted.Length - 33];
   305	            Array.Copy(encrypted, 33, ciphertext, 0, ciphertext.Length);
   306	
   307	            byte[] encapped = Crypto.UncompressRawPublicKey(compressedSender);
   308	
   309	            byte[] decrypted = Crypto.HpkeDecrypt(new Crypto.HpkeDecryptParams
   310	            {
   311	                CiphertextBuf = ciphertext,
   312	                EncappedKeyBuf = encapped,
   313	                ReceiverPriv = recv.PrivateKey,
   314	            });
   315	
   316	            decrypted.Should().Equal(payload);
   317	        }
   318	
   319	        [Fact]
   320	        public void Hpke_EncryptThenDecrypt_EmptyPayload()
   321	        {
   322	            var recv = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   323	            byte[] recvPubUncompressed = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(recv.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   324	
   325	            byte[] encrypted = Crypto.HpkeEncrypt(new Crypto.HpkeEncryptParams
   326	            {
   327	                PlainTextBuf = Array.Empty<byte>(),
   328	                TargetKeyBuf = recvPubUncompressed,
   329	            });
   330	
   331	            var compressedSender = new byte[33];
   332	            Array.Copy(encrypted, 0, compressedSender, 0, 33);
   333	            var ciphertext = new byte[encrypted.Length - 33];
   334	            Array.Copy(encrypted, 33, ciphertext, 0, ciphertext.Length);
   335	
   336	            byte[] decrypted = Crypto.HpkeDecrypt(new Crypto.HpkeDecryptParams
   337	            {
   338	                CiphertextBuf = ciphertext,
   339	                EncappedKeyBuf = Crypto.UncompressRawPublicKey(compressedSender),
   340	                ReceiverPriv = recv.PrivateKey,
   341	            });
   342	            decrypted.Should().BeEmpty();
   343	        }
   344	
   345	        [Fact]
   346	        public void Hpke_DecryptWithWrongKey_Throws()
   347	        {
   348	            var recv = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   349	            var attacker = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   350	            byte[] recvPubUncompressed = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(recv.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   351	
   352	            byte[] encrypted = Crypto.HpkeEncrypt(new Crypto.HpkeEncryptParams
   353	            {
   354	                PlainTextBuf = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString("aabbcc"),
   355	                TargetKeyBuf = recvPubUncompressed,
   356	            });
   357	
   358	            var compressedSender = new byte[33];
   359	            Array.Copy(encrypted, 0, compressedSender, 0, 33);
   360	            var ciphertext = new byte[encrypted.Length - 33];

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba tests/Fixtures/README.md | sed -n '1,240p'" in /Users/takeshi/Kyuzan/src/turnkey-sdk-csharp
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Test fixtures
     2	
     3	Each subdirectory groups fixtures for one production file, plus a
     4	provenance note explaining where the bytes came from.
     5	
     6	## Provenance levels
     7	
     8	- **upstream-test-vectors** — directly copied from the upstream
     9	  `__tests__/` directory inside `codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`.
    10	- **rfc** — standardized test vectors (RFC 5869, RFC 9180, etc.).
    11	- **nist** — NIST CAVP P-256 vectors.
    12	- **turnkey-sample** — sample bundles published by Turnkey in their
    13	  developer docs (de-identified).
    14	- **node-generated** — bytes generated by running the pinned upstream Node
    15	  package inside `tests/Fixtures/Generators/`. The generator script,
    16	  Node version, and output hash are recorded so reviewers can re-run.
    17	
    18	## Layout
    19	
    20	```
    21	Fixtures/
    22	├── encoding/
    23	│   └── turnkey-encoding-vectors.json       — upstream-test-vectors
    24	├── crypto/                                  — (added with Crypto.cs port)
    25	├── api-key-stamper/                         — (added with ApiKeyStamper.cs port)
    26	└── http/                                    — (added with Http.cs port)
    27	```
    28	
    29	## Re-generating
    30	
    31	Fixtures sourced from upstream test files are stable as long as
    32	[`codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/`](../../codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/)
    33	is not bumped. If a pin is bumped, re-extract the upstream `__tests__/`
    34	into the matching fixture file and update its sha256 in
    35	`tarball-checksums.txt` next to the new tarball.

./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:676:codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/package/CHANGELOG.md:1:# @turnkey/crypto
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:677:codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/package/CHANGELOG.md:37:- [#1050](https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/pull/1050) [`c745646`](https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/commit/c745646ae4b2a275e116abca07c6e108f89beb04) Author [@amircheikh](https://github.com/amircheikh) - - Removed `@peculiar/webcrypto` dependancy. This will fix build errors in environments where `webcrypto` is not defined but will still require a polyfill if you use a function where `webcrypto` is required.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:678:codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/package/CHANGELOG.md:153:- [#812](https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/pull/812) [`6cde41c`](https://github.com/tkhq/sdk/commit/6cde41cfecdfb7d54abf52cc65e28ef0e2ad6ba3) Author [@turnekybc](https://github.com/turnekybc) - Add `@turnkey/encoding` as a package dependency instead of a devDependency to `@turnkey/crypto`. This resolves an issue with transitive dependencies when devDependencies are not included in the artifact.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r3-20260523.md:679:codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/package/CHANGELOG.md:185:- 2bc0046: Migrated from WebCrypto (crypto.subtle.verify) to Noble for ECDSA signature verification

./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4857:    Fix Crypto.cs divergences flagged by Codex r1
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4859:    Codex r1 (codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md) found 15
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4868:    2. DecryptCredentialBundle Base58 fallback (BEHAVIOR-CRITICAL):
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4874:    3. DecryptExportBundle legacy envelope (BEHAVIOR-CRITICAL): the
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4880:       EncryptPrivateKeyToBundleParams + DecryptExportBundleParams. Wired
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4884:       parameter to VerifySessionJwtSignature.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4890:    7. HpkeEncrypt / HpkeDecrypt error wrapping: both now wrap
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4891:       exceptions as "Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt: ..." /
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4892:       "Unable to perform hpkeDecrypt: ..." per upstream try/catch.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4894:    8. VerifySessionJwtSignature: upstream throws
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4899:    9. EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle ordering: DecodeKey now runs BEFORE
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4902:    10. Required non-empty organizationId: DecryptExportBundle now
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4910:    12. FormatHpkeBuf: delegate length validation to UncompressRawPublicKey
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4916:        (rejection sampling) to randomBytes(32) + GetPublicKey, matching
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4933:    - VerifySessionJwtSignature_Empty / WrongPartCount now expect throws
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4936:    - New DecryptCredentialBundle_RawBase58_Rejected test.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4940:    - FormatHpkeBuf_TooSmall error message updated.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:4945:    Codex r1 evidence committed as codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md.

./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5430:5085:| `src/Crypto.cs:151` | `ts-source/math.ts:8` | Public negative `x` differs: C# `BigInteger.Mod` normalizes non-negative; JS `%` keeps negative sign. Crypto call sites use non-negative EC coordinates, but the public helper is observably different. | Mirror JS remainder for public parity or make helper private/document non-negative input. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5431:5086:| `src/Crypto.cs:355` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:358-363` | C# uses `ECKeyPairGenerator`, which samples valid scalars; TS samples 32 random bytes then derives public key. Rare invalid scalar/failure/distribution behavior differs. | If strict parity is required, generate 32 random bytes and derive with the same validation behavior; otherwise document as deliberate hardening. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5432:5087:| `src/Crypto.cs:435-438` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:95-151` | `HpkeEncrypt` treats null `PlainTextBuf` as empty and does not wrap errors as `Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt`. TS catches and wraps all errors. | Require non-null plaintext and wrap exceptions with the upstream message. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5433:5088:| `src/Crypto.cs:386-421` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:308-350` | `HpkeDecrypt` throws C# argument/crypto exceptions directly; TS wraps all errors as `Unable to perform hpkeDecrypt: ...`. | Wrap non-fatal exceptions with upstream-equivalent message. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5434:5089:| `src/Crypto.cs:482-486` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:406-418` | C# rejects non-65-byte or non-`0x04` input; TS slices any input and mutates prefix based on last byte. Valid public keys match, malformed input behavior differs. | Remove extra validation for parity or keep and document stricter validation. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5435:5090:| `src/Crypto.cs:506` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:426-462` | C# only supports P-256; upstream exposes optional `curve` and supports `CURVE_SECP256K1`. | Add curve parameter/support or document intentional P-256-only subset. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5436:5091:| `src/Crypto.cs:509-512` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:430-432` | Invalid compressed length error message differs. | Use upstream message if exact error parity matters. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5437:5092:| `src/Crypto.cs:579-582` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:286-299` | C# rejects buffers `<= 33`; TS slices first 33 bytes and allows downstream behavior. A valid compressed key plus empty ciphertext would stringify upstream but throw in C#. | Match TS slicing behavior, or only reject `< 33` if defensive validation is desired. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5438:5093:| `src/Crypto.cs:614-619` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:76-77` | C# falls back from Base58Check to raw Base58; upstream only accepts Base58Check. This can accept bundles TS rejects. | Remove raw Base58 fallback. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5439:5094:| `src/Crypto.cs:276-283`, `289-296`, `851` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:31-45`, `116-123`, `444-450` | DTO/API shape omits `dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey` and `dangerouslyOverrideNotarizerPublicKey`. | Add optional override fields/parameters and pass them through to verification helpers. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5440:5095:| `src/Crypto.cs:665-708` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:325-360` | `EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle` verifies/parses bundle before `DecodeKey`; upstream decodes private key before signature verification. Error precedence differs when both inputs are bad. | Move `DecodeKey` immediately after JSON parse to match upstream. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5441:5096:| `src/Crypto.cs:753-767`, `1052` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:124-162` | C# accepts a legacy `signature`/`signedData` envelope path not present in upstream 2.8.8. | Remove from this port or isolate as a separately named compatibility helper. |

./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5475:5183:| `src/Crypto.cs:151` | `ts-source/math.ts:8` | Public negative `x` differs: C# `BigInteger.Mod` normalizes non-negative; JS `%` keeps negative sign. Crypto call sites use non-negative EC coordinates, but the public helper is observably different. | Mirror JS remainder for public parity or make helper private/document non-negative input. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5476:5184:| `src/Crypto.cs:355` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:358-363` | C# uses `ECKeyPairGenerator`, which samples valid scalars; TS samples 32 random bytes then derives public key. Rare invalid scalar/failure/distribution behavior differs. | If strict parity is required, generate 32 random bytes and derive with the same validation behavior; otherwise document as deliberate hardening. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5477:5185:| `src/Crypto.cs:435-438` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:95-151` | `HpkeEncrypt` treats null `PlainTextBuf` as empty and does not wrap errors as `Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt`. ./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5478:5186:| `src/Crypto.cs:386-421` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:308-350` | `HpkeDecrypt` throws C# argument/crypto exceptions directly; TS wraps all errors as `Unable to perform hpkeDecrypt: ...`. | Wrap non-fatal exceptions with upstream-equivalent message. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5479:5187:| `src/Crypto.cs:482-486` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:406-418` | C# rejects non-65-byte or non-`0x04` input; TS slices any input and mutates prefix based on last byte. ./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5482:5190:| `src/Crypto.cs:579-582` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:286-299` | C# rejects buffers `<= 33`; TS slices first 33 bytes and allows downstream behavior. A valid compressed key plus empty ciphertext would stringify upstream but throw in C#. | Match TS slicing behavior, or only reject `< 33` if defensive validation is desired. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5483:5191:| `src/Crypto.cs:614-619` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:76-77` | C# falls back from Base58Check to raw Base58; upstream only accepts Base58Check. This can accept bundles TS rejects. | Remove raw Base58 fallback. |

./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5536:- `System.Text.Json` source generation replaces `JSON.stringify` for `FormatHpkeBuf`: property names/order match `encappedPublic`, then `ciphertext`.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5545:Crypto.cs D17 result: no banned HPKE/KDF API string is present.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5547:- No `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Generators.HkdfBytesGenerator`.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5548:- No `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Hpke.*`.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5550:- BouncyCastle is used for ECDH, AES-GCM, ECDSA, BigInteger, EC point operations, and also `Ed25519PrivateKeyParameters` at `src/Crypto.cs:819`. That Ed25519 use is a primitive for Solana export parity, but it is outside the exact D17 allowed list as written.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5556:| `src/Crypto.cs:168` | `ts-source/math.ts:8` | Negative public `x` differs: C# `BigInteger.Mod` normalizes to non-negative; JS `%` keeps the negative sign. Production EC call sites pass non-negative values. | Use JS-style remainder for exact public helper parity, or make the helper private/non-negative-only. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5557:| `src/Crypto.cs:459` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:95-138` | `HpkeEncrypt` treats `PlainTextBuf = null` as empty plaintext; TS would fail when `plainTextBuf` is absent/invalid. | Require non-null plaintext and let the upstream-style catch wrap the error. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5558:| `src/Crypto.cs:520-523` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:412-418` | Empty input to `CompressRawPublicKey` throws in C#; TS returns an empty `Uint8Array` because out-of-bounds typed-array assignment is ignored. | Special-case `rawPublicKey.Length == 0` to return `Array.Empty<byte>()`. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5559:| `src/Crypto.cs:690-698` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:325-327` | `EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle` pre-validates required strings before JSON parse/decode; TS parses `importBundle` then decodes `privateKey`, so malformed multi-error inputs have different error precedence. | Move/remove pre-validation to mirror TS operation order if exact error parity is required. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5560:| `src/Crypto.cs:758-763` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:124-126` | `DecryptExportBundle` pre-validates params before `JSON.parse`; TS enters the try and parses first. Error precedence/message differ for malformed inputs. | Parse first inside the try, then follow upstream field checks. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5561:| `src/Crypto.cs:1054-1056` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:126-135`, `ts-source/turnkey.ts:327-335` | Invalid enclave signature throws `"failed to verify enclave signature"` inside the helper; TS helper returns `false`, and callers throw messages containing the bundle context. | Return `bool` from `VerifyEnclaveSignature` and let callers throw the upstream contextual errors. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5565:No fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` exercises `src/Crypto.cs`. The only fixture present is `tests/Fixtures/encoding/turnkey-encoding-vectors.json`, which is for `Encoding.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` mentions a future `crypto/` fixture directory, but it is not present.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5567:Result: fixture gate is not satisfiable as “all fixtures match” for `Crypto.cs`; there are no pinned Node-generated Crypto fixtures to compare. Existing `tests/CryptoTests.cs` uses RFC literals, NIST-style known values, negative cases, and randomized C# roundtrips, not pinned upstream fixture files.

./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5632:Crypto.cs D17 result: no banned HPKE/KDF API string is present.

./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5637:- BouncyCastle is used for ECDH, AES-GCM, ECDSA, BigInteger, EC point operations, and also `Ed25519PrivateKeyParameters` at `src/Crypto.cs:819`. That Ed25519 use is a primitive for Solana export parity, but it is outside the exact D17 allowed list as written.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5643:| `src/Crypto.cs:168` | `ts-source/math.ts:8` | Negative public `x` differs: C# `BigInteger.Mod` normalizes to non-negative; JS `%` keeps the negative sign. Production EC call sites pass non-negative values. | Use JS-style remainder for exact public helper parity, or make the helper private/non-negative-only. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5644:| `src/Crypto.cs:459` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:95-138` | `HpkeEncrypt` treats `PlainTextBuf = null` as empty plaintext; TS would fail when `plainTextBuf` is absent/invalid. | Require non-null plaintext and let the upstream-style catch wrap the error. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5645:| `src/Crypto.cs:520-523` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:412-418` | Empty input to `CompressRawPublicKey` throws in C#; TS returns an empty `Uint8Array` because out-of-bounds typed-array assignment is ignored. | Special-case `rawPublicKey.Length == 0` to return `Array.Empty<byte>()`. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5646:| `src/Crypto.cs:690-698` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:325-327` | `EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle` pre-validates required strings before JSON parse/decode; TS parses `importBundle` then decodes `privateKey`, so malformed multi-error inputs have different error precedence. | Move/remove pre-validation to mirror TS operation order if exact error parity is required. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5647:| `src/Crypto.cs:758-763` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:124-126` | `DecryptExportBundle` pre-validates params before `JSON.parse`; TS enters the try and parses first. Error precedence/message differ for malformed inputs. | Parse first inside the try, then follow upstream field checks. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r2-20260523.md:5648:| `src/Crypto.cs:1054-1056` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:126-135`, `ts-source/turnkey.ts:327-335` | Invalid enclave signature throws `"failed to verify enclave signature"` inside the helper; TS helper returns `false`, and callers throw messages containing the bundle context. | Return `bool` from `VerifyEnclaveSignature` and let callers throw the upstream contextual errors. |

./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5066:- `System.Text.Json` source generation replaces `JSON.stringify` for `HpkeBundlePayload`; property names/order are explicit and unindented.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5074:Crypto.cs check: no banned API present.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5076:- No `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Generators.HkdfBytesGenerator`.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5077:- No `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Hpke.*`.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5079:- BouncyCastle usage is limited to EC key/point/ECDH/ECDSA, AES-GCM, SHA-256 signing mode, BigInteger, and related primitive parameter types. The broad `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Generators` using is used for `ECKeyPairGenerator`, not HKDF.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5085:| `src/Crypto.cs:151` | `ts-source/math.ts:8` | Public negative `x` differs: C# `BigInteger.Mod` normalizes non-negative; JS `%` keeps negative sign. Crypto call sites use non-negative EC coordinates, but the public helper is observably different. | Mirror JS remainder for public parity or make helper private/document non-negative input. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5086:| `src/Crypto.cs:355` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:358-363` | C# uses `ECKeyPairGenerator`, which samples valid scalars; TS samples 32 random bytes then derives public key. Rare invalid scalar/failure/distribution behavior differs. | If strict parity is required, generate 32 random bytes and derive with the same validation behavior; otherwise document as deliberate hardening. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5087:| `src/Crypto.cs:435-438` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:95-151` | `HpkeEncrypt` treats null `PlainTextBuf` as empty and does not wrap errors as `Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt`. TS catches and wraps all errors. | Require non-null plaintext and wrap exceptions with the upstream message. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5088:| `src/Crypto.cs:386-421` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:308-350` | `HpkeDecrypt` throws C# argument/crypto exceptions directly; TS wraps all errors as `Unable to perform hpkeDecrypt: ...`. | Wrap non-fatal exceptions with upstream-equivalent message. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5089:| `src/Crypto.cs:482-486` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:406-418` | C# rejects non-65-byte or non-`0x04` input; TS slices any input and mutates prefix based on last byte. Valid public keys match, malformed input behavior differs. | Remove extra validation for parity or keep and document stricter validation. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5090:| `src/Crypto.cs:506` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:426-462` | C# only supports P-256; upstream exposes optional `curve` and supports `CURVE_SECP256K1`. | Add curve parameter/support or document intentional P-256-only subset. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5091:| `src/Crypto.cs:509-512` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:430-432` | Invalid compressed length error message differs. | Use upstream message if exact error parity matters. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5092:| `src/Crypto.cs:579-582` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:286-299` | C# rejects buffers `<= 33`; TS slices first 33 bytes and allows downstream behavior. A valid compressed key plus empty ciphertext would stringify upstream but throw in C#. | Match TS slicing behavior, or only reject `< 33` if defensive validation is desired. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5093:| `src/Crypto.cs:614-619` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:76-77` | C# falls back from Base58Check to raw Base58; upstream only accepts Base58Check. This can accept bundles TS rejects. | Remove raw Base58 fallback. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5094:| `src/Crypto.cs:276-283`, `289-296`, `851` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:31-45`, `116-123`, `444-450` | DTO/API shape omits `dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey` and `dangerouslyOverrideNotarizerPublicKey`. | Add optional override fields/parameters and pass them through to verification helpers. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5095:| `src/Crypto.cs:665-708` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:325-360` | `EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle` verifies/parses bundle before `DecodeKey`; upstream decodes private key before signature verification. Error precedence differs when both inputs are bad. | Move `DecodeKey` immediately after JSON parse to match upstream. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5096:| `src/Crypto.cs:753-767`, `1052` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:124-162` | C# accepts a legacy `signature`/`signedData` envelope path not present in upstream 2.8.8. | Remove from this port or isolate as a separately named compatibility helper. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5097:| `src/Crypto.cs:796-803` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:144-151` | C# allows missing `organizationId`; upstream rejects missing or mismatched `organizationId`. | Require `bundleOrgId` to be non-empty and equal. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5098:| `src/Crypto.cs:827-830` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:164-180` | SOLANA export output is wrong: C# Base58-encodes only 32 decrypted private-key bytes; upstream derives Ed25519 public key, concatenates 32 private + 32 public bytes, then Base58-encodes 64 bytes. | Derive Ed25519 public key and encode the 64-byte concatenation. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5099:| `src/Crypto.cs:853-890` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:452-477` | Malformed JWT behavior differs: C# returns `false` for wrong part count and catches all errors; upstream throws for missing signature part and returns verifier result otherwise. | Throw on malformed JWT and avoid catch-all if exact upstream behavior is required. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5103:Not satisfied for `Crypto.cs`.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5105:`tests/Fixtures/` currently contains only `tests/Fixtures/encoding/turnkey-encoding-vectors.json`; no fixture under `tests/Fixtures/` exercises `src/Crypto.cs`. `tests/Fixtures/README.md` lists `crypto/` as planned, but the directory is absent. `tests/CryptoTests.cs` uses inline RFC/NIST/random roundtrip tests and explicitly states positive Turnkey-signed bundle tests are not present.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5107:So I cannot confirm that every Crypto.cs fixture was generated from the pinned upstream package or that C# asserts the same bytes Node would produce. There are no applicable Crypto fixtures to compare.

./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5172:Crypto.cs check: no banned API present.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5174:- No `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Generators.HkdfBytesGenerator`.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5175:- No `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Hpke.*`.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5177:- BouncyCastle usage is limited to EC key/point/ECDH/ECDSA, AES-GCM, SHA-256 signing mode, BigInteger, and related primitive parameter types. The broad `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Generators` using is used for `ECKeyPairGenerator`, not HKDF.
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5183:| `src/Crypto.cs:151` | `ts-source/math.ts:8` | Public negative `x` differs: C# `BigInteger.Mod` normalizes non-negative; JS `%` keeps negative sign. Crypto call sites use non-negative EC coordinates, but the public helper is observably different. | Mirror JS remainder for public parity or make helper private/document non-negative input. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5184:| `src/Crypto.cs:355` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:358-363` | C# uses `ECKeyPairGenerator`, which samples valid scalars; TS samples 32 random bytes then derives public key. Rare invalid scalar/failure/distribution behavior differs. | If strict parity is required, generate 32 random bytes and derive with the same validation behavior; otherwise document as deliberate hardening. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5185:| `src/Crypto.cs:435-438` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:95-151` | `HpkeEncrypt` treats null `PlainTextBuf` as empty and does not wrap errors as `Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt`. TS catches and wraps all errors. | Require non-null plaintext and wrap exceptions with the upstream message. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5186:| `src/Crypto.cs:386-421` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:308-350` | `HpkeDecrypt` throws C# argument/crypto exceptions directly; TS wraps all errors as `Unable to perform hpkeDecrypt: ...`. | Wrap non-fatal exceptions with upstream-equivalent message. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5187:| `src/Crypto.cs:482-486` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:406-418` | C# rejects non-65-byte or non-`0x04` input; TS slices any input and mutates prefix based on last byte. Valid public keys match, malformed input behavior differs. | Remove extra validation for parity or keep and document stricter validation. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5188:| `src/Crypto.cs:506` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:426-462` | C# only supports P-256; upstream exposes optional `curve` and supports `CURVE_SECP256K1`. | Add curve parameter/support or document intentional P-256-only subset. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5189:| `src/Crypto.cs:509-512` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:430-432` | Invalid compressed length error message differs. | Use upstream message if exact error parity matters. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5190:| `src/Crypto.cs:579-582` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:286-299` | C# rejects buffers `<= 33`; TS slices first 33 bytes and allows downstream behavior. A valid compressed key plus empty ciphertext would stringify upstream but throw in C#. | Match TS slicing behavior, or only reject `< 33` if defensive validation is desired. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5191:| `src/Crypto.cs:614-619` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:76-77` | C# falls back from Base58Check to raw Base58; upstream only accepts Base58Check. This can accept bundles TS rejects. | Remove raw Base58 fallback. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5192:| `src/Crypto.cs:276-283`, `289-296`, `851` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:31-45`, `116-123`, `444-450` | DTO/API shape omits `dangerouslyOverrideSignerPublicKey` and `dangerouslyOverrideNotarizerPublicKey`. | Add optional override fields/parameters and pass them through to verification helpers. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5193:| `src/Crypto.cs:665-708` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:325-360` | `EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle` verifies/parses bundle before `DecodeKey`; upstream decodes private key before signature verification. Error precedence differs when both inputs are bad. | Move `DecodeKey` immediately after JSON parse to match upstream. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5194:| `src/Crypto.cs:753-767`, `1052` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:124-162` | C# accepts a legacy `signature`/`signedData` envelope path not present in upstream 2.8.8. | Remove from this port or isolate as a separately named compatibility helper. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5195:| `src/Crypto.cs:796-803` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:144-151` | C# allows missing `organizationId`; upstream rejects missing or mismatched `organizationId`. | Require `bundleOrgId` to be non-empty and equal. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5196:| `src/Crypto.cs:827-830` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:164-180` | SOLANA export output is wrong: C# Base58-encodes only 32 decrypted private-key bytes; upstream derives Ed25519 public key, concatenates 32 private + 32 public bytes, then Base58-encodes 64 bytes. | Derive Ed25519 public key and encode the 64-byte concatenation. |
./codex-crypto-reviews/Crypto.cs-r1-20260523.md:5197:| `src/Crypto.cs:853-890` | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:452-477` | Malformed JWT behavior differs: C# returns `false` for wrong part count and catches all errors; upstream throws for missing signature part and returns verifier result otherwise. | Throw on malformed JWT and avoid catch-all if exact upstream behavior is required. |

./codex-crypto-reviews/Encoding.cs-r1-20260523.md:308:src/turnkey-sdk-csharp.csproj:12:    <Description>Unofficial / community-maintained Turnkey SDK for .NET by Kyuzan Inc. Not affiliated with Turnkey, Inc. 1:1 logical port of @turnkey/crypto v2.8.8, @turnkey/http v3.16.0, @turnkey/api-key-stamper v0.5.0, and @turnkey/encoding v0.6.0 (peak-pinned versions). Provides P-256 ECDSA / HPKE / HKDF / API key stamping primitives for .NET. BouncyCastle 2.5.0 is used only for primitives; HPKE / HKDF / bundle parsing are direct ports of the upstream Turnkey TypeScript logic.</Description>

./codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-http-3.16.0/package/dist/base.js:53:        // https://node.green/#ES2022-features-Error-cause-property
./codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-http-3.16.0/package/CHANGELOG.md:295:  - This release introduces an `invalidateExisting` flag to the `CreateReadWriteSession` and `Oauth` activities. If enabled, this will invalidate existing read-write and oauth API keys. This is useful in scenarios where a user attempts to create numerous `ReadWrite` or `Oauth` sessions. Because our API caps the number of session keys associated with a user, this flag can clear all other existing session keys of that specific type (e.g. setting `invalidateExisting: true` for `CreateReadWriteSession` will invalidate all previously created read-write session keys)
