   372	        // ============================================================
   373	        // BuildAdditionalAssociatedData / FormatHpkeBuf
   374	        // ============================================================
   375	
   376	        [Fact]
   377	        public void BuildAdditionalAssociatedData_ConcatsArgs()
   378	        {
   379	            byte[] a = { 0x01, 0x02 };
   380	            byte[] b = { 0x03, 0x04, 0x05 };
   381	            byte[] aad = Crypto.BuildAdditionalAssociatedData(a, b);
   382	            aad.Should().Equal(new byte[] { 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 });
   383	        }
   384	
   385	        [Fact]
   386	        public void FormatHpkeBuf_ReturnsExpectedJson()
   387	        {
   388	            // Use a real HpkeEncrypt output so the bytes are valid.
   389	            var recv = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   390	            byte[] recvPubUncompressed = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(recv.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   391	
   392	            byte[] encrypted = Crypto.HpkeEncrypt(new Crypto.HpkeEncryptParams
   393	            {
   394	                PlainTextBuf = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString("1234"),
   395	                TargetKeyBuf = recvPubUncompressed,
   396	            });
   397	
   398	            string json = Crypto.FormatHpkeBuf(encrypted);
   399	
   400	            // Shape contains exactly two keys in upstream order.
   401	            json.Should().StartWith("{\"encappedPublic\":\"04");
   402	            json.Should().Contain("\"ciphertext\":\"");
   403	            json.Should().EndWith("\"}");
   404	        }
   405	
   406	        [Fact]
   407	        public void FormatHpkeBuf_TooSmall_Throws()
   408	        {
   409	            // Upstream passes the slice to uncompressRawPublicKey which
   410	            // throws "failed to uncompress raw public key: invalid length"
   411	            // when the buffer is shorter than 33 bytes.
   412	            Action act = () => Crypto.FormatHpkeBuf(new byte[10]);
   413	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
   414	               .WithMessage("failed to uncompress raw public key: invalid length");
   415	        }
   416	
   417	        // ============================================================
   418	        // VerifySessionJwtSignature negative paths
   419	        // ============================================================
   420	
   421	        [Fact]
   422	        public void VerifySessionJwtSignature_Empty_Throws()
   423	        {
   424	            // Upstream: throws "invalid JWT: need 3 parts" when signature part is missing.
   425	            Action act = () => Crypto.VerifySessionJwtSignature(string.Empty);
   426	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
   427	               .WithMessage("invalid JWT: need 3 parts");
   428	        }
   429	
   430	        [Fact]
   431	        public void VerifySessionJwtSignature_WrongPartCount_Throws()
   432	        {
   433	            Action act = () => Crypto.VerifySessionJwtSignature("just.two");
   434	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
   435	               .WithMessage("invalid JWT: need 3 parts");
   436	        }
   437	
   438	        [Fact]
   439	        public void VerifySessionJwtSignature_BadSignatureLength_ReturnsFalse()
   440	        {
   441	            // 3 parts but the signature decodes to a non-64-byte buffer.
   442	            string jwt = "aGVhZGVy.cGF5bG9hZA.YWJj"; // last part decodes to "abc" (3 bytes)
   443	            Crypto.VerifySessionJwtSignature(jwt).Should().BeFalse();
   444	        }
   445	
   446	        [Fact]
   447	        public void VerifySessionJwtSignature_WellFormedButForgedSig_ReturnsFalse()
   448	        {
   449	            // 64-byte signature of all zeros — should fail verification.
   450	            string header = Encoding.Base64StringToBase64UrlEncodedString(
   451	                Convert.ToBase64String(System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes("{\"alg\":\"ES256\"}")));
   452	            string payload = Encoding.Base64StringToBase64UrlEncodedString(
   453	                Convert.ToBase64String(System.Text.Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes("{\"sub\":\"x\"}")));
   454	            string sig = Encoding.Base64StringToBase64UrlEncodedString(
   455	                Convert.ToBase64String(new byte[64]));
   456	            string jwt = header + "." + payload + "." + sig;
   457	            Crypto.VerifySessionJwtSignature(jwt).Should().BeFalse();
   458	        }
   459	
   460	        // ============================================================
   461	        // Curve.Secp256k1 — UncompressRawPublicKey
   462	        // ============================================================
   463	
   464	        [Fact]
   465	        public void UncompressRawPublicKey_Secp256k1_Roundtrip()
   466	        {
   467	            // secp256k1 generator point compressed = 02 79be667e f9dcbbac 55a06295 ce870b07
   468	            //                                       029bfcdb 2dce28d9 59f2815b 16f81798
   469	            byte[] compressed = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(
   470	                "0279be667ef9dcbbac55a06295ce870b07029bfcdb2dce28d959f2815b16f81798");
   471	            byte[] uncompressed = Crypto.UncompressRawPublicKey(compressed, Crypto.Curve.Secp256k1);
   472	            uncompressed.Should().HaveCount(65);
   473	            uncompressed[0].Should().Be(0x04);
   474	            // Y coordinate of G must be even (prefix was 0x02).
   475	            (uncompressed[64] & 1).Should().Be(0);
   476	        }
   477

   479	        // Bundle helpers — including a real Turnkey-pinned vector

   481	
   482	        [Fact]
   483	        public void DecryptCredentialBundle_UpstreamVector()
   484	        {
   485	            // From upstream tests/__tests__/crypto-test.ts:179-184
   486	            // (codex-crypto-reviews/upstream-snapshots/turnkey-crypto-2.8.8/ts-source/__tests__/crypto-test.ts)
   487	            const string credentialBundle =
   488	                "w99a5xV6A75TfoAUkZn869fVyDYvgVsKrawMALZXmrauZd8hEv66EkPU1Z42CUaHESQjcA5bqd8dynTGBMLWB9ewtXWPEVbZvocB4Tw2K1vQVp7uwjf";
   489	            const string embeddedKey =
   490	                "20fa65df11f24833790ae283fc9a0c215eecbbc589549767977994dc69d05a56";
   491	            const string expectedSenderPrivateKey =
   492	                "67ee05fc3bdf4161bc70701c221d8d77180294cefcfcea64ba83c4d4c732fcb9";
   493	
   494	            string decrypted = Crypto.DecryptCredentialBundle(credentialBundle, embeddedKey);
   495	            decrypted.Should().Be(expectedSenderPrivateKey);
   496	        }
   497	
   498	        [Fact]
   499	        public void UncompressRawPublicKey_UpstreamInvalidPrefixVector()
   500	        {
   501	            // From upstream tests/__tests__/crypto-test.ts:243-250
   502	            byte[] invalidPrefix = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(
   503	                "77c6047f9441ed7d6d3045406e95c07cd85c778e4b8cef3ca7abac09b95c709ee5");
   504	            Action act = () => Crypto.UncompressRawPublicKey(invalidPrefix);
   505	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>()
   506	               .WithMessage("failed to uncompress raw public key: invalid prefix");
   507	        }
   508	
   509	        [Fact]
   510	        public void CompressRawPublicKey_EmptyInput_ReturnsEmpty()
   511	        {
   512	            // Upstream behavior: empty Uint8Array slice produces empty result.
   513	            Crypto.CompressRawPublicKey(Array.Empty<byte>()).Should().BeEmpty();
   514	        }
   515	
   516	        [Fact]
   517	        public void HpkeEncrypt_NullPlainTextBuf_Throws()
   518	        {
   519	            // Upstream throws when plainTextBuf is absent (wrapped via try/catch).
   520	            var recv = Crypto.GenerateP256KeyPair();
   521	            byte[] recvPubUncompressed = Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(recv.PublicKeyUncompressed);
   522	            Action act = () => Crypto.HpkeEncrypt(new Crypto.HpkeEncryptParams
   523	            {
   524	                PlainTextBuf = null,
   525	                TargetKeyBuf = recvPubUncompressed,
   526	            });
   527	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
   528	               .WithMessage("Unable to perform hpkeEncrypt:*");
   529	        }
   530	
   531	        [Fact]
   532	        public void DecryptCredentialBundle_BundleTooSmall_Throws()
   533	        {
   534	            // Base58Check of a small (<33 byte) payload should still decode (with valid checksum)
   535	            // but trigger the size guard. Use Base58Check encode so DecryptCredentialBundle's
   536	            // bs58check decode succeeds, then it should fail on the size check.
   537	            string tiny = Encoding.Base58CheckEncode(new byte[] { 0x01, 0x02, 0x03 });
   538	            Action act = () => Crypto.DecryptCredentialBundle(tiny, new string('1', 64));
   539	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>()
   540	               .WithMessage("*Error decrypting bundle*");
   541	        }
   542	
   543	        [Fact]
   544	        public void DecryptCredentialBundle_RawBase58_Rejected()
   545	        {
   546	            // Upstream uses bs58check.decode exclusively. A plain base58 input
   547	            // (no checksum) must NOT be accepted.
   548	            string rawBs58 = Encoding.Base58Encode(new byte[40]);
   549	            Action act = () => Crypto.DecryptCredentialBundle(rawBs58, new string('1', 64));
   550	            act.Should().Throw<InvalidOperationException>();
   551	        }
   552	
   553	        [Fact]
   554	        public void EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle_MissingArgs_Throws()
   555	        {
   556	            Action act = () => Crypto.EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle(new Crypto.EncryptPrivateKeyToBundleParams());
   557	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>();
   558	        }
   559

   561	        public void DecryptExportBundle_MissingArgs_Throws()
   562	        {
   563	            Action act = () => Crypto.DecryptExportBundle(new Crypto.DecryptExportBundleParams());
   564	            act.Should().Throw<ArgumentException>();
   565	        }

   572	        {
   573	            return Encoding.Uint8ArrayFromHexString(hex);

   577	        {

    30	describe("HPKE Encryption and Decryption", () => {
    31	  test("hpkeAuthEncrypt and hpkeDecrypt - end-to-end encryption and decryption", () => {
    32	    const senderKeyPair = generateP256KeyPair();
    33	    const receiverKeyPair = generateP256KeyPair();
    34	    const receiverPublicKeyUncompressed = uncompressRawPublicKey(
    35	      uint8ArrayFromHexString(receiverKeyPair.publicKey),
    36	    );
    37	
    38	    const textEncoder = new TextEncoder();
    39	    // Mock plaintext
    40	    const plainText = "Hello, this is a secure message!";
    41	    const plainTextBuf = textEncoder.encode(plainText);
    42	    // Encrypt
    43	    const encryptedDataBuf = hpkeAuthEncrypt({
    44	      plainTextBuf: plainTextBuf,
    45	      targetKeyBuf: receiverPublicKeyUncompressed,
    46	      senderPriv: senderKeyPair.privateKey,
    47	    });
    48	    const encryptedData = formatHpkeBuf(encryptedDataBuf);
    49	    // Extract the encapsulated key buffer and the ciphertext
    50	    const data = JSON.parse(encryptedData);
    51	    // Decrypt
    52	    const decryptedData = hpkeDecrypt({
    53	      ciphertextBuf: uint8ArrayFromHexString(data.ciphertext),
    54	      encappedKeyBuf: uint8ArrayFromHexString(data.encappedPublic),
    55	      receiverPriv: receiverKeyPair.privateKey,
    56	    });
    57	
    58	    // Convert decrypted data back to string
    59	    const decryptedText = new TextDecoder().decode(decryptedData);
    60	
    61	    // Expect the decrypted text to equal the original plaintext
    62	    expect(decryptedText).toEqual(plainText);
    63	  });
    64	});
    65	
    66	describe("HPKE Standard Encryption and Decryption", () => {
    67	  test("hpkeEncrypt and hpkeDecrypt - standard mode (ephemeral sender key)", async () => {
    68	    // Generate a receiver key pair
    69	    const receiverKeyPair = generateP256KeyPair();
    70	    const receiverPublicKeyUncompressed = uncompressRawPublicKey(
    71	      uint8ArrayFromHexString(receiverKeyPair.publicKey),
    72	    );
    73	
    74	    // Prepare the plaintext
    75	    const textEncoder = new TextEncoder();
    76	    const plainText =
    77	      "6ab33bd6e4bdc73017233da0554f9616fe10ede5c3ce001e81b321d5a74199b7";
    78	    const plainTextBuf = textEncoder.encode(plainText);
    79	
    80	    // Encrypt using standard mode (no sender private key provided)
    81	    const encryptedDataBuf = hpkeEncrypt({
    82	      plainTextBuf: plainTextBuf,
    83	      targetKeyBuf: receiverPublicKeyUncompressed,
    84	      // No senderPriv provided, so it will use an ephemeral key
    85	    });
    86	    const encryptedData = formatHpkeBuf(encryptedDataBuf);
    87	    // Parse the encrypted data
    88	    const data = JSON.parse(encryptedData);
    89	    // Decrypt the message
    90	    const decryptedData = hpkeDecrypt({
    91	      ciphertextBuf: uint8ArrayFromHexString(data.ciphertext),
    92	      encappedKeyBuf: uint8ArrayFromHexString(data.encappedPublic),
    93	      receiverPriv: receiverKeyPair.privateKey,
    94	    });
    95	
    96	    // Convert decrypted data back to string
    97	    const decryptedText = new TextDecoder().decode(decryptedData);
    98	
    99	    // Verify that the decrypted text matches the original plaintext
   100	    expect(decryptedText).toEqual(plainText);
   101	
   102	    // Additional checks to ensure standard mode behavior
   103	    const encappedPublicKey = uint8ArrayFromHexString(data.encappedPublic);
   104	    expect(encappedPublicKey.length).toBe(65); // Uncompressed public key length
   105	    expect(encappedPublicKey[0]).toBe(0x04); // Uncompressed public key prefix
   106	  });
   107	});

   153	describe("Turnkey Crypto Primitives", () => {
   154	  test("getPublicKey - returns the correct public key", () => {

   163	  test("generateP256KeyPair - generates a valid key pair", () => {
   164	    const keyPair = generateP256KeyPair();
   165	    expect(keyPair.privateKey).toBeTruthy();
   166	    expect(keyPair.publicKey).toBeTruthy();
   167	    expect(keyPair.publicKeyUncompressed).toBeTruthy();
   168	    expect(keyPair.privateKey).not.toEqual(keyPair.publicKey);
   169	    expect(keyPair.publicKey).not.toEqual(keyPair.publicKeyUncompressed);
   170	  });
   171

   199	  test("verifyRequestStamp", async () => {
   200	    const { publicKey: apiPublicKey, privateKey: apiPrivateKey } =

   225	      signature,
   226	      stampedRequest.body,
   227	    );

   230	  });

   262	  describe("Valid DER signatures", () => {
   263	    test("should parse a simple DER signature with short-form length", () => {
   264	      // Create a signature with 32-byte r and s values
   265	      const rValue = new Array(32).fill(0x01);

   270

   272	    });

   275	      // ECDSA signatures sometimes have leading zeros to ensure positive integers

   277	      const sValue = [0x00, ...new Array(32).fill(0x90)];
   278	      const totalLength = 2 + 33 + 2 + 33;

   280	      const derHex = createDerSignature([totalLength], rValue, sValue);

   282	      expect(() => fromDerSignature(derHex)).not.toThrow();

   284	  });

---
name: codex-review
version: 2.1.4
author: BenedictKing
description: "Professional code review skill for Claude Code. Automatically collects file changes and task status. Triggers when working directory has uncommitted changes, or reviews latest commit when clean. Triggers: code review, review, 代码审核, 代码审查, 检查代码"
allowed-tools:
  - Task
  - Bash
  - Read
  - Glob
  - Write
  - Edit
user-invocable: true
---

# Codex Code Review Skill

## Trigger Conditions

Triggered when user input contains:

- "代码审核", "代码审查", "审查代码", "审核代码"
- "review", "code review", "review code", "codex 审核"
- "帮我审核", "检查代码", "审一下", "看看代码"

## Core Concept: Intention vs Implementation

Running `codex review --uncommitted` alone only shows AI "what was done (Implementation)".
Recording intention first tells AI "what you wanted to do (Intention)".

**"Code changes + intention description" as combined input is the most effective way to improve AI code review quality.**

## Skill Architecture

This skill operates in two phases:

1. **Preparation Phase** (current context): Check working directory, update CHANGELOG
2. **Review Phase** (isolated context): Invoke Task tool to execute Lint + codex review (using context: fork to reduce context waste)

## Execution Steps

### 0. [First] Check Working Directory Status

```bash
git diff --name-only && git status --short
```

**Decide review mode based on output:**

- **Has uncommitted changes** → Continue with steps 1-4 (normal flow)
- **Clean working directory** → Directly invoke codex-runner: `codex review --commit HEAD`

### 1. [Mandatory] Check if CHANGELOG is Updated

**Before any review, must check if CHANGELOG.md contains description of current changes.**

```bash
# Check if CHANGELOG.md is in uncommitted changes
git diff --name-only | grep -E "(CHANGELOG|changelog)"
```

**If CHANGELOG is not updated, you must automatically perform the following (don't ask user to do it manually):**

1. **Analyze changes**: Run `git diff --stat` and `git diff` to get complete changes
2. **Auto-generate CHANGELOG entry**: Generate compliant entry based on code changes
3. **Write to CHANGELOG.md**: Use Edit tool to insert entry at top of `[Unreleased]` section
4. **Continue review flow**: Immediately proceed to next steps after CHANGELOG update

**Auto-generated CHANGELOG entry format:**

```markdown
## [Unreleased]

### Added / Changed / Fixed

- Feature description: what problem was solved or what functionality was implemented
- Affected files: main modified files/modules
```

**Example - Auto-generation Flow:**

```
1. Detected CHANGELOG not updated
2. Run git diff --stat, found handlers/responses.go modified (+88 lines)
3. Run git diff to analyze details: added CompactHandler function
4. Auto-generate entry:
   ### Added
   - Added `/v1/responses/compact` endpoint for conversation context compression
   - Supports multi-channel failover and request body size limits
5. Use Edit tool to write to CHANGELOG.md
6. Continue with lint and codex review
```

### 2. [Critical] Stage All New Files

**Before invoking codex review, must add all new files (untracked files) to git staging area, otherwise codex will report P1 error.**

```bash
# Check for new files
git status --short | grep "^??"
```

**If there are new files, automatically execute:**

```bash
# Safely stage all new files (handles empty list and special filenames)
git ls-files --others --exclude-standard -z | while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do git add -- "$f"; done
```

**Explanation:**

- `-z` uses null character to separate filenames, correctly handles filenames with spaces/newlines
- `while IFS= read -r -d ''` reads filenames one by one
- `git add -- "$f"` uses `--` separator, correctly handles filenames starting with `-`
- When no new files exist, loop body doesn't execute, safely skipped
- This won't stage modified files, only handles new files
- codex needs files to be tracked by git for proper review

### 3. Evaluate Task Difficulty and Invoke codex-runner

**Count change scale:**

```bash
# Count number of changed files and lines of code
git diff --stat | tail -1
```

**Difficulty Assessment Criteria:**

**Difficult Tasks** (meets any condition):

- Modified files ≥ 10
- Total code changes (insertions + deletions) ≥ 500 lines
- Single metric: insertions ≥ 300 lines OR deletions ≥ 300 lines
- Involves core architecture/algorithm changes
- Cross-module refactoring
- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=xhigh`, timeout 30 minutes

**Normal Tasks** (other cases):

- Config: `model_reasoning_effort=high`, timeout 10 minutes

**Evaluation Method:**

You MUST parse the `git diff --stat` output correctly to determine difficulty:

```bash
# Get the summary line (last line of git diff --stat)
git diff --stat | tail -1
# Example outputs:
# "20 files changed, 342 insertions(+), 985 deletions(-)"
# "1 file changed, 50 insertions(+)"  # No deletions
# "3 files changed, 120 deletions(-)"  # No insertions
```

**Parsing Rules:**
1. Extract file count from "X file(s) changed" (handle both "1 file" and "N files")
2. Extract insertions from "Y insertion(s)(+)" if present (handle both "1 insertion" and "N insertions"), otherwise 0
3. Extract deletions from "Z deletion(s)(-)" if present (handle both "1 deletion" and "N deletions"), otherwise 0
4. Calculate total changes = insertions + deletions

**Important Edge Cases:**
- Single file: `"1 file changed"` (singular form)
- No insertions: Git omits `"insertions(+)"` entirely → treat as 0
- No deletions: Git omits `"deletions(-)"` entirely → treat as 0
- Pure rename: May show `"0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-)"` or omit both

**Decision Logic (ANY condition triggers xhigh):**
- IF file_count >= 10 → xhigh
- IF total_changes >= 500 → xhigh
- IF insertions >= 300 → xhigh
- IF deletions >= 300 → xhigh
- ELSE → high

**Example Cases:**
- ✅ "20 files changed, 342 insertions(+), 985 deletions(-)" → xhigh (files=20≥10, total=1327≥500, deletions=985≥300)
- ✅ "5 files changed, 600 insertions(+), 50 deletions(-)" → xhigh (total=650≥500, insertions=600≥300)
- ✅ "12 files changed, 100 insertions(+), 50 deletions(-)" → xhigh (files=12≥10)
- ✅ "1 file changed, 400 deletions(-)" → xhigh (deletions=400≥300)
- ❌ "3 files changed, 150 insertions(+), 80 deletions(-)" → high (all conditions fail)
- ❌ "1 file changed, 50 insertions(+)" → high (no deletions, total=50<500)

**Invoke codex-runner Subtask:**

Use Task tool to invoke codex-runner, passing complete command (including Lint + codex review):

     1	/**
     2	 * HKDF (RFC 5869): extract + expand in one step.
     3	 * See https://soatok.blog/2021/11/17/understanding-hkdf/.
     4	 * @module
     5	 */
     6	import { ahash, anumber } from './_assert.ts';
     7	import { hmac } from './hmac.ts';
     8	import { type CHash, type Input, toBytes } from './utils.ts';
     9	
    10	/**
    11	 * HKDF-extract from spec. Less important part. `HKDF-Extract(IKM, salt) -> PRK`
    12	 * Arguments position differs from spec (IKM is first one, since it is not optional)
    13	 * @param hash - hash function that would be used (e.g. sha256)
    14	 * @param ikm - input keying material, the initial key
    15	 * @param salt - optional salt value (a non-secret random value)
    16	 */
    17	export function extract(hash: CHash, ikm: Input, salt?: Input): Uint8Array {
    18	  ahash(hash);
    19	  // NOTE: some libraries treat zero-length array as 'not provided';
    20	  // we don't, since we have undefined as 'not provided'
    21	  // https://github.com/RustCrypto/KDFs/issues/15
    22	  if (salt === undefined) salt = new Uint8Array(hash.outputLen);
    23	  return hmac(hash, toBytes(salt), toBytes(ikm));
    24	}
    25	
    26	const HKDF_COUNTER = /* @__PURE__ */ new Uint8Array([0]);
    27	const EMPTY_BUFFER = /* @__PURE__ */ new Uint8Array();
    28	
    29	/**
    30	 * HKDF-expand from the spec. The most important part. `HKDF-Expand(PRK, info, L) -> OKM`
    31	 * @param hash - hash function that would be used (e.g. sha256)
    32	 * @param prk - a pseudorandom key of at least HashLen octets (usually, the output from the extract step)
    33	 * @param info - optional context and application specific information (can be a zero-length string)
    34	 * @param length - length of output keying material in bytes
    35	 */
    36	export function expand(hash: CHash, prk: Input, info?: Input, length: number = 32): Uint8Array {
    37	  ahash(hash);
    38	  anumber(length);
    39	  if (length > 255 * hash.outputLen) throw new Error('Length should be <= 255*HashLen');
    40	  const blocks = Math.ceil(length / hash.outputLen);
    41	  if (info === undefined) info = EMPTY_BUFFER;
    42	  // first L(ength) octets of T
    43	  const okm = new Uint8Array(blocks * hash.outputLen);
    44	  // Re-use HMAC instance between blocks
    45	  const HMAC = hmac.create(hash, prk);
    46	  const HMACTmp = HMAC._cloneInto();
    47	  const T = new Uint8Array(HMAC.outputLen);
    48	  for (let counter = 0; counter < blocks; counter++) {
    49	    HKDF_COUNTER[0] = counter + 1;
    50	    // T(0) = empty string (zero length)
    51	    // T(N) = HMAC-Hash(PRK, T(N-1) | info | N)
    52	    HMACTmp.update(counter === 0 ? EMPTY_BUFFER : T)
    53	      .update(info)
    54	      .update(HKDF_COUNTER)
    55	      .digestInto(T);
    56	    okm.set(T, hash.outputLen * counter);
    57	    HMAC._cloneInto(HMACTmp);
    58	  }
    59	  HMAC.destroy();
    60	  HMACTmp.destroy();
    61	  T.fill(0);
    62	  HKDF_COUNTER.fill(0);
    63	  return okm.slice(0, length);
    64	}

    66	/**
    67	 * HKDF (RFC 5869): derive keys from an initial input.
    68	 * Combines hkdf_extract + hkdf_expand in one step

    70	 * @param ikm - input keying material, the initial key

    77	 * import { randomBytes } from '@noble/hashes/utils';

| `EncryptPrivateKeyToBundle` (`src/Crypto.cs:698`) | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:317` | REVIEWED | Parse/verify/check/encrypt order reviewed. |
| `DecryptExportBundle` (`src/Crypto.cs:778`) | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:116` | REVIEWED | SOLANA/mnemonic/hex branches reviewed. |
| `VerifySessionJwtSignature` (`src/Crypto.cs:886`) | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:444` | REVIEWED | Double-SHA256 and raw `r||s` reviewed. |
| `DeriveSS` (`src/Crypto.cs:923`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:559` | REVIEWED | Private-key validation divergence in E. |
| `GetKemContext` (`src/Crypto.cs:949`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:598` | REVIEWED | Concatenation matches. |
| `BuildLabeledIkm` (`src/Crypto.cs:956`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:491` | REVIEWED | HPKE label layout matches. |
| `BuildLabeledInfo` (`src/Crypto.cs:961`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:524` | REVIEWED | Length/version/suite/label/info layout matches. |
| `ExtractAndExpand` (`src/Crypto.cs:977`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:545` | REVIEWED | HKDF call order matches. |
| `AesGcmDecrypt` (`src/Crypto.cs:983`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:584` | REVIEWED | AES-GCM tag layout reviewed. |
| `AesGcmEncrypt` (`src/Crypto.cs:994`) | `ts-source/crypto.ts:570` | REVIEWED | AES-GCM tag layout reviewed. |
| `Base64UrlDecode` (`src/Crypto.cs:1005`) | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:462` | REVIEWED | JWT signature decode helper. |
| `VerifyP256RawSignature` (`src/Crypto.cs:1016`) | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:477` | REVIEWED | Raw compact signature converted to DER for BouncyCastle verify. |
| `VerifyEnclaveSignature` (`src/Crypto.cs:1054`) | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:234` | REVIEWED | DER ECDSA verification reviewed. |
| `DecodeKey` (`src/Crypto.cs:1105`) | `ts-source/turnkey.ts:284` | REVIEWED | SOLANA/HEXADECIMAL/default branches reviewed. |
| `GetStringOrNull` (`src/Crypto.cs:1128`) | Dynamic JSON property access in `ts-source/turnkey.ts:125`, `337` | REVIEWED | Structural helper for TS object access. |

**C. Intentional Adaptations**
- C# `byte[]` plus `Array.Copy` replaces `Uint8Array.slice/set/spread`; structural byte movement only.
- PascalCase DTOs replace TS object literals; JSON names preserve upstream wire fields where serialized.
- `System.Text.Json` source generation replaces `JSON.parse/stringify`; HPKE output remains `encappedPublic` then `ciphertext`.
- `Curve` enum replaces TS string union; only dispatch representation changes.
- `GetPublicKey(string)` overload replaces TS `Uint8Array | string` union; intended same hex input behavior.
- BouncyCastle primitives replace noble/WebCrypto primitives; HPKE/HKDF orchestration remains manually ported.
- Sync C# methods replace async TS wrappers where no C# async operation is needed; output bytes unchanged.
- `SecureRandom` replaces noble `randomBytes`; cryptographic randomness role is equivalent.
- Ed25519 seed-to-public derivation uses BouncyCastle for the SOLANA export branch; intended same 32-byte public key.

**D. D17 Enforcement**
- Banned APIs are not present: no `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Generators.HkdfBytesGenerator`, no `Org.BouncyCastle.Crypto.Hpke.*`, and no high-level KDF/HPKE wrapper use.
- Caveat: `src/Crypto.cs:851` uses `Ed25519PrivateKeyParameters` for the SOLANA `decryptExportBundle` branch. That is a primitive replacement for upstream `ed25519.getPublicKey`, but it is outside the D17 allow-list exactly as written.

**E. Logic Divergence Findings**

| C# file:line | TS upstream path:line | Semantic diff | Suggested fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/Crypto.cs:162` | `ts-source/math.ts:4` | `ModSqrt` normalizes negative `x` with `BigInteger.Mod`; TS uses BigInt `%`, so negative inputs can throw upstream but succeed in C#. | Reject `x < 0` or implement JS remainder semantics before the square-root check. |
| `src/Crypto.cs:344`, `src/Crypto.cs:364` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:80` | C# accepts non-32-byte private key inputs and does not explicitly reject scalar `0` / `>= n`; noble `p256.getPublicKey` validates exact key size and scalar range. | Add a shared P-256 private-key validator: exactly 32 bytes and scalar in `[1, n - 1]`. |
| `src/Crypto.cs:923` | `ts-source/crypto.ts:559` | Same private-key validation gap exists in ECDH shared-secret derivation; upstream delegates to `p256.getSharedSecret`, which validates the private scalar. | Reuse the same validator before constructing `ECPrivateKeyParameters`. |

**F. Fixture Comparison Gate**
- `tests/Fixtures/crypto/turnkey-crypto-vectors.json` is pinned to `@turnkey/crypto@2.8.8` and the same tarball SHA above.
- Its credential bundle vector cites `ts-source/__tests__/crypto-test.ts:179-184`; `tests/CryptoTests.cs:483-495` asserts the same decrypted bytes.
- Its invalid-prefix vector cites `ts-source/__tests__/crypto-test.ts:243-250`; `tests/CryptoTests.cs:499-506` asserts the same error message.
- Its invalid-length/roundtrip coverage maps to `tests/CryptoTests.cs:184-232` and `510-513`.
- Gate result by source inspection: all Crypto fixtures match the pinned upstream source vectors. The encoding fixture does not exercise `Crypto.cs`.

**G. Unresolved Assumptions**
- I did not run `dotnet test`; this read-only sandbox blocks .NET temp directory creation.
- I could not execute the pinned Node package directly because the extracted tarball does not include its dependencies, so fixture validation here is source-level against the pinned upstream snapshot.
